name: Ascent to Cerro de San Blas — round trip from Baterno
name_original: Subida al Cerro de San Blas — ida y vuelta desde Baterno
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Puebla Alcocer are situated in the Extremadura region of Spain, within La Siberia Extremadura, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. The area is characterized by diverse natural landscapes, including the Sierra de Alcocer mountains and extensive plains. It is surrounded by large reservoirs such as La Serena and García de Sola, formed by the Zújar and Guadiana rivers, which create aquatic landscapes amidst Mediterranean forests. The terrain features rocky crests and hills, offering varied environments for outdoor exploration. Best easy hiking trails around Puebla Alcocer The most popular easy hiking route is Sierra Brava Reservoir Trail, a 2.7 miles (4.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 10 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Sierra Brava Reservoir and surrounding natural areas. Another top favourite among local hikers is Alto de las Poyatas Trail, an easy 3.5 miles (5.6 km) path. This trail features varied terrain with an elevation gain of 749 feet (228 meters), providing expansive views of the landscape. Local hikers also love the Peñón de Pez Trail, a 2.5 miles (4.1 km) trail leading through natural surroundings, often completed in about 1 hour 16 minutes. Hiking around Puebla Alcocer is defined by reservoir landscapes, Mediterranean forests, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, with 12 easy hiking trails available. The routes in Puebla Alcocer are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Puebla Alcocer's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available around Puebla Alcocer?
A: There are 12 easy hiking trails around Puebla Alcocer, offering diverse landscapes from reservoir views to Mediterranean forests. These routes are suitable for various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on easy hikes in Puebla Alcocer?
A: Easy hikes in Puebla Alcocer feature stunning reservoir landscapes, particularly around the vast La Serena and García de Sola reservoirs. You'll also encounter Mediterranean forests, rolling hills, and rocky crests, characteristic of the La Siberia Extremadura region.

Q: Are there any easy circular walks in the area?
A: Yes, there are several easy circular walks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24527384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa de La Moheda Alta loop&lt;/a&gt; is about 5 km long, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31428657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrera del Duque Town Center – Herrera del Duque loop&lt;/a&gt; is slightly over 5 km, both offering pleasant, manageable distances.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions to see on easy hikes?
A: You can explore significant natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334082" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garcia de Sola Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334061" target="_noopener"&gt;Masatrigo Mountain and La Serena Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, known for its rich birdlife.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks accessible via easy trails?
A: Yes, the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5460742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt;, perched atop a rocky crest, is a significant historical landmark. While a historical route leads up to it, many easy trails offer panoramic views of the castle and its surroundings.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in Puebla Alcocer?
A: The easy trails in Puebla Alcocer are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the beautiful reservoir views and the serene natural environment.

Q: What is a good easy trail for views of the reservoirs?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990700585/sierra-brava-reservoir-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Brava Reservoir Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This 4.3 km route offers direct views of the Sierra Brava Reservoir and the surrounding natural areas, making it perfect for enjoying the aquatic landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy trails with some elevation for broader views?
A: For expansive views with a manageable ascent, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293807664/alto-de-las-poyatas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de las Poyatas Trail&lt;/a&gt;. This 5.6 km path features an elevation gain of 228 meters, providing sweeping vistas of the landscape without being overly strenuous.

Q: What is the typical duration for an easy hike in Puebla Alcocer?
A: Most easy hikes in Puebla Alcocer can be completed within 1 to 2 hours. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990700585/sierra-brava-reservoir-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Brava Reservoir Trail&lt;/a&gt; takes about 1 hour 10 minutes, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1294150597/penon-de-pez-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de Pez Trail&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 1 hour 16 minutes.

Q: Can I find easy trails that are relatively flat?
A: Yes, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24527384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa de La Moheda Alta loop&lt;/a&gt; offer minimal elevation changes, making them ideal for those seeking a flatter, less challenging walk through the dehesa landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for easy hiking in Puebla Alcocer?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for hiking in Puebla Alcocer. The weather is milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant, offering comfortable conditions for exploring the trails and enjoying the region's beauty.

Q: Are there any easy trails near the Orellana Reservoir?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Orellana loop from Orellana la Vieja&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.9 km trail that allows you to explore the surroundings of the Orellana Reservoir, offering pleasant views of the water and nearby areas.
tips: To the south of Cerro de San Blas we find Baterno, which is a small population center that is part of the commonwealth of La Siberia Extremeña.
This is one of the paths that takes you in the direction of the top of Cerro de San Blas and that is further west and where the old path that took you from Agudo to Baterno is.
